reviews.review_msg: Dr. Chauve was trying to cover as much material as possible and sometimes, it was hard to understand the def w/out instant examples. The stuff can be a bit challenging. Definitely go to his office hours. He would answer your questions and help with your hw. Be prepared for some French accent but it's still understandable. Generally, a nice prof!!

He's unorganized sometimes but his lecture is pretty good. His material is easy to follow (not saying this course is easy, but at least it makes it easier) and he would give extra credit for finding a bug in his assignment. Just devote your time in it and you'll get good grades easily.

This professor is very disorganized. They don't upload materials properly or on time. They often come late to class and don't teach the material clearly. The questions they give on tests are much harder than what they teach in class, and their notes are very hard to understand. Overall, the class is very confusing, and I have struggled a lot.

Nice guy, but didn't return a single grade back for 2 + 1/4 months. Lecture pace is an absolute mess (1-4 for midterm 1, 5-17 for midterm 2), which heavily impacted midterm studying and assignments. HW 1,2 was for the first midterm, and only had HW 3 for the second midterm that went up to lecture 7 ---> 10 lectures no practice. Please avoid him.

I took Combinatorics/Graph Theory from Prof. Hell 40 years ago, and it was (really!) life-changing. What I learned, esp. from programming assignments, made things easy in my 1st job as a database professional. More important, I learned teaching techniques I use all the time, and I ran business process imprvmt workshops on five continents last year.
